author: aLiEn_sPoReZ
content:
If you are trying to figure out how a set or rims and tires will fit in your wheel wells, here are some simple examples to help you decide which rim width and offset will look best. I've printed this out and hung it by my computer and as I shop for rims on eBay I can quickly see if a set or rims will be too wide for my fender opening.

My calculations are for 18" rims only, because that's what I intend to put on my Tib, but the diameter shouldn't matter so these should be valid for all diameters. I highly recommend the following link found by Solo2Tib BEST RIM SIZE/OFFSET CALCULATOR to also help you decide.

STOCK RIMS: 17" x 7" Offset 46mm
STOCK TIRES: 215/45-17
STOCK DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 1.7"
STOCK DISTANCE FROM THE HUB TO FENDER LIP: 2.7"

The stock rim/tire setup leaves slightly less than a 1" gap between the rim lip and the inside of the fender lip. So, using some basic math, you will figure out that the distance from the hub to the inside of the fender lip (drawing a perfectly vertical line) is about 2.7". Taking into account that the width of the tire wall is about 0.5".

The rims you choose have to have a "DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP" value of 2.2" or less. Anything more and you might get some rubbing during hard cornering (unless you have a widebody kit).

Rolling your fender lips (or cutting them off) will give you an additional 0.5" of clearance, meaning the distance from the hub to inside of the fender lip (drawing a perfectly vertical line) will be 2.7" or less.

18" x 7.5" Offset 45mm
215/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 1.95"

18" x 7.5" Offset 42mm (Most common size for Tibs)
215/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 2.05"

18" x 7.5" Offset 40mm
215/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 2.15"

18" x 7.5" Offset 30mm
215/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 2.35" Possible Rub

18" x 8" Offset 45mm
215/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 2.2"

18" x 8" Offset 40mm
215/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 2.4" Possible Rub

18" x 8" Offset 35mm
215/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 2.6" Rub, unless rolled fender

18" x 8.5" Offset 35mm
225/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 2.85" Rub, unless rolled fender

18" x 8.5" Offset 30mm
225/40-18 (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 3.05" RUB

18" x 9" Offset 35mm
??? (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 3.1" RUB

18" x 9" Offset 30mm
??? (Suggested Tire size)
DISTANCE FROM HUB TO WHEEL RIM LIP: 3.3" RUB

Hope this helps!

Also see Post #2 for the info created by STAW512

author: staw512
content:
The NEW "Does it fit" thread

Here is a bunch of information on the tiburons lug pattern, and what size rims, tires and drops will work.

The Tiburon's bolt pattern is 5-114.3
Stock Rims: 17" x 7" Offset 46mm
Stock Tires: 215/45-17
Stock distance from hub to wheel rim lip: 1.7"
Stock distance from hub to fender lip: 2.7"

Wheel . . . Offset . . .Tire . . . Notes

Stock Height

17x7.0 +35 215/45/17, 225/45/17, 245/45/17(slight rub in F)
17x7.5 +38 225/45/17
17x7.5 +40 235/45/17, 225/45/17
17x8.0 +42 245/40/17
17x9.0 +45 255/40/17(R only w/ 5mm spacer and rolled fender, still slight rub)
18x7.5 +38 225/40/18
18x7.5 +40 235/40/18
18x7.5 +45 225/40/18
18x8.0 +35 225/40/18
18x8.0 +46 225/40/18
18x8.5 +35 225/40/18
19x8.0 +40 245/35/19
19x10. +25 275/40/19(R only, w/ widebody and fender roll, slight rub)
19x9.0 +50 245/35/19(w/ widebody, slight rub F)

Pro-Kit (or equivalent)

18x8.0 +40 225/40/18, 235/40/18(slight rub)
18x8.0 +45 245/35/18(rolled R fender, slight rub R)

Sportline (or equivalent)

17x7.0 +38 235/40/17
17x7.5 +40 225/45/17
18x8.0 +?? 235/40/18(bad rub and fitment)

Tein S-tech (or equivalent)

17x7.0 +45 225/45/17
18x7.5 +45 225/40/18
18x8.0 +35 225/40/18

Tein Coilovers (or equivalent)

18x8.0 +35 225/40/18(set hard or will rub slightly)

Espelir (or equivalent)

18x7.5 +30 235/40/18(slight rub F)

Tanabe DF210 (or equivalent)

19x7.5 +45 235/35/19

B & G (or equivalent)

18x7.5 +40 225/45/18

Dropzone (or equivalent)

18x7.5 +40-45 225/40/18(slight rub, on bumps only)

author: kagame
content:
ok, just got some 18x 8.5 +35 on 225/40/18s i got yokohama es100 on the fronts and nitto 555s on the back. the nittos round out a bit more than the es100s and it sorta helps w/ the rubbing. im on tein ssp coilovers and my front and backs are dropped pretty low, i have a no finger gap on the back and a 1 finger gap on the fronts. no rub on front however slight rub on the back when i hit big bumps and when entering or exiting angled drive ways. i have my rear shocks set at ultra stiff (16 clicks clockwise) my fenders are trimmed and the rub is caused by the inner plastic inside wheel well and a small screw near the top left of the inside the wheel well as also. if the inner plastic and that screw is taken out its possible to not rub at all.

ok thats just my 2cents =p

author: GTV6TIB
content:
those will fit, but it will be a little ''stretched"

if you like the meatier look, get 215 or 225 width...

good brand name tire also, but they're neo gens, meaning they might be a little soft, plus the treadwear is only 280......

author: Tuscanirider
content:
The tread life index (treadwear) measures the relative tread life of the tyre compared to a "government reference". An index of 100 is equivalent to an estimated tread life of 30,000 miles of highway driving. and thats to be less expected life than a tire of 200 treadwear.

280 as a figure is among the high ones, but as far as i knew you should also consider that if the treadwear figure is too high (high resistance to wear) it has counter effect on grip

author: Tuscanirider
content: you did the homework for most of us aLiEn, thanks

author: SlovenianTuscani
content:
18" x 8" Offset 35mm stock suspension slight rub only in the rear when cornering hard with somebody sitting in the rear seats. There is no rubbing in front and if there are just two or one occupant in the car and no rubbing even when cornering hard with just two or one occupants.
To get rid of the rubbing in the rear just cut the fender flares for 1/4" and you're good to go with no rubbing.
That's my experience.
